meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell with this expert-led portfolio building guide. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your SaaS MVP and generate real revenue.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By building a high-impact, revenue-focused portfolio, you transition from 'student' to 'founder.' Use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to accelerate your implementation, focusing exclusively on solving tangible user pain points to ensure market validation.
content.intro:
You are trapped in a loop of endless documentation and polished YouTube demos, building apps that no one uses and solving problems that don't exist. The screen glows, the code compiles, but the bank account stays empty. You aren't learning; you are consuming digital comfort food while your competitors are shipping real value to paying users.

It is time to dismantle the tutorial crutch. Real engineering isn't about following steps—it's about architectural decision-making under pressure. By building a portfolio of lean, production-ready SaaS tools, you move beyond syntax and into the realm of product-market fit. This isn't about code quality for the sake of beauty; it's about speed, utility, and the relentless pursuit of revenue.

steps.title: Define the Revenue-First MVP
steps.description: Identify a singular, painful problem in a niche market. Do not build a platform; build a utility. Define the core loop that provides immediate value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following problem space and architect a lean MVP schema that addresses only the core pain point. Ignore features that don't directly lead to user retention.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a project roadmap focusing on the single most valuable feature for a [Niche] user.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate your boilerplate, ensuring your stack is production-ready, scalable, and devoid of tutorial-bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade boilerplate for a [Type of App] using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Focus on clean separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project with the scaffolding provided by Antigravity and configure environment variables.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Logic with Claude
steps.description: Don't write boilerplate. Use Claude Code to build the core business logic, forcing it to focus on edge cases and error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my business logic requirements and write the core service layer, focusing on atomic operations and database safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core business logic from the spec provided in the Antigravity session.'

steps.title: Build for Monetization
steps.description: Integrate payments immediately. If you aren't charging, you aren't building a product; you're building a hobby.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe integration flow that handles user subscriptions and webhook event processing for my MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add Stripe checkout flow and webhook handler to the current codebase.'

steps.title: Ship to Production
steps.description: Deploy to a live URL within 48 hours. The feedback loop is the only metric that matters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for Vercel/Railway including security headers and database optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify build integrity and generate deployment configuration files.'

pro_tips: Never build an auth system from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20 hours of dev time.
The 80/20 rule: 80% of your users will only use 20% of your features. Ruthlessly cut the rest.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your checkout flow first; if the money doesn't move, the app doesn't exist.
Documentation is for post-launch; focus your energy on writing clean, readable code that explains itself.
Market validation happens in the DM's, not the GitHub repo. Share your build-in-public journey to get your first 10 users.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, pivot from building to distribution. Reach out to 50 potential users in your niche, gather feedback, and iterate on the core feature based on their actual usage patterns.
content.conversion_block: Stop watching others build and start claiming your share of the market. Your first profitable SaaS is one weekend of focused execution away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
